canonical_definition: The degree to which a large language model is likely to accept and incorporate false information provided by a user, even when it contradicts the model's internal knowledge.
papers: Know Your Mistakes: Towards Preventing Overreliance on Task-Oriented ConversationalAIThrough Accountability Modeling
Scaling Laws and Efficient Inference for Ternary Language Models

definitions.text: The degree to which a large language model is likely to accept and incorporate false information provided by a user, even when it contradicts the model's internal knowledge.
definitions.paper: Scaling Laws and Efficient Inference for Ternary Language Models

definitions.text: The tendency of a large language model to favor knowledge provided by a human user over conflicting information from a database, even when the user's information is factually incorrect.
definitions.paper: Know Your Mistakes: Towards Preventing Overreliance on Task-Oriented ConversationalAIThrough Accountability Modeling

characterization: The degree to which a large language model is likely to accept and incorporate false information provided by a user, even when it contradicts the model's internal knowledge.
